message:
perf(core): wrap ToolResult in Arc and adopt Cow-based Context to eliminate hot-path cloning

The agent loop's per-turn overhead was dominated by deep-cloning tool result
messages (which often contain large file contents from read operations) and
rebuilding the full conversation history + tool definition vectors on every
provider call. This commit attacks both bottlenecks:

**Arc<ToolResultMessage> wrapping (src/model.rs, src/session.rs):**
- Message::ToolResult now holds Arc<ToolResultMessage> instead of owned value
- Added convenience constructor Message::tool_result() that wraps in Arc
- Session serialization updated to unwrap Arc via as_ref()/Arc::clone()
- Enables cheap reference-counted sharing during event dispatch and persistence

**Cow-based Context (src/provider.rs, src/agent.rs):**
- Context struct now uses Cow<'a, [Message]> and Cow<'a, [ToolDef]>
- build_context() returns Context<'_> with borrowed lifetimes (zero-copy common case)
- Only allocates owned copies when block_images config is enabled
- Added Context::owned() constructor for callers with already-owned data

**Tool definition caching (src/agent.rs):**
- Introduced cached_tool_defs field to avoid rebuilding tool defs every turn
- Cache invalidated on extend_tools() calls
- build_tool_defs() now takes &mut self to manage cache lifecycle

**Mechanical test adaptations across the suite:**
- All ToolResult pattern matches updated: Some(r) → Some(r.as_ref())
- All ToolResult constructors updated: Message::ToolResult(...) → Message::tool_result(...)
- Provider test helpers wrap in Arc::new() directly where needed

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>
